serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one large bowl, approximately 550 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Noodle soup, Vietnamese, beef (pho)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) entry for 'Noodle soup, Vietnamese, beef (pho)' (FDC ID 173877), with adjustments made to protein, fat, and sodium to reflect the 'Signature Combination' with BBQ pork, quail eggs, and mixed seafood, which typically has a richer and more varied protein and fat content.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli noodles (cooked)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is estimated based on the GI of cooked rice vermicelli noodles (average 58) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The presence of protein, fat, and other ingredients in the soup may slightly modify the overall glycemic response, but the noodles are the primary carbohydrate source.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth and seasonings.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its sodium content, or request a reduced-sodium version if available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index from rice vermicelli and carbohydrate content.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Portion control is important for managing blood glucose levels; consider increasing vegetable intake and reducing noodle portion to lower the glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from BBQ pork and sodium content.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Consume in moderation, focusing on lean protein components and limiting intake of fatty pork cuts and high-sodium broth to support cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Signature Combination Vietnamese Rice Vermicelli Soup is a hearty and flavorful noodle soup featuring a rich broth, thin rice vermicelli noodles, and a variety of toppings. This particular combination often includes succulent BBQ pork, tender quail eggs, and a medley of fresh seafood such as shrimp, squid, and fish cake. The broth is typically simmered for hours with pork bones and aromatic spices, yielding a naturally sweet and savory base. The dish is commonly garnished with fresh herbs like cilantro, basil, and mint, along with bean sprouts and lime, adding a vibrant freshness and textural contrast. Nutritionally, it provides a good source of protein from the meat and seafood, carbohydrates from the noodles, and some fiber from the vegetables. Due to its sodium content and medium glycemic index, individuals managing hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes should consider portion sizes and ingredient choices.
common_user_queries: Nutritional value of Vietnamese vermicelli soup
Recipe for Bún Thịt Nướng Trứng Cút Hải Sản

confidence_reason: Confidence is medium due to the need to adapt nutrient values from a similar Vietnamese noodle soup (beef pho) and estimate the glycemic index based on the primary carbohydrate component (rice vermicelli). The exact combination dish was not found in primary nutrient databases.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, vitamin B1, vitamin B2, vitamin B3, vitamin B6, vitamin B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are -1 as they are not reported in the source database for the analogous food. The input 'cuisine' was corrected from 'Singapore' to 'Vietnamese' based on the food name ('越南米粉') and restaurant name ('Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt'). The Chinese name in the input mentioned 'beef' (牛肉) while the English mentioned 'BBQ Pork'; the Chinese name was preserved as an alias, and the English description was prioritized for the canonical food name and Vietnamese local name.
